meta.title: Sushi Making in Calgary | Mixler
meta.description: Learn to make sushi in Calgary at a fun, hands-on class. Roll, slice, and eat your own creations. No experience needed. Great for groups and date nights.

content.subtitle: Roll, slice, and eat the sushi you make yourself.
content.intro: Sushi making classes in Calgary are a crowd favourite for good reason. Learning to prepare and roll sushi is a skill that takes an hour to pick up and a lifetime to perfect, and a guided class gives you a genuine head start. You work with fresh ingredients, learn the proper technique from an experienced instructor, and eat very well at the end.

what_to_expect.heading: Sushi rice preparation
what_to_expect.body: You'll learn the ratio and seasoning for proper sushi rice. It's the foundation everything else is built on.

what_to_expect.heading: Rolling technique
what_to_expect.body: The instructor shows you how to build and roll maki rolls using a bamboo mat. Tight rolling takes practice but you'll get it.

what_to_expect.heading: Slicing and plating
what_to_expect.body: Clean cuts matter. You'll learn how to slice rolls properly and plate them so they look as good as they taste.

what_to_expect.heading: Eat your creations
what_to_expect.body: Everything you make is for eating. Expect a full spread by the time you're done, plus plenty of leftovers.

tips: Don't overfill your roll. The most common mistake is packing too much filling, which makes rolling and cutting much harder.
Wet your knife blade between cuts. A clean blade makes the difference between a neat slice and a crushed roll.
Calgary has excellent Japanese grocery stores where you can pick up sushi-grade fish to practice at home after the class.
A sushi class is a fantastic date activity. Working together on something hands-on and a little tricky makes for great conversation.
Mixler lists sushi making events in Calgary when they're scheduled. Join the waitlist and we'll email you when one is added.
content.waitlist_heading: Want to know when we run sushi making events?
content.waitlist_body: Join the waitlist and we'll email you when we add one. We use this to plan what to run next.

faq.q: Do I need any cooking experience?
faq.a: No experience needed. The class starts from the very beginning, including how to prepare the rice.

faq.q: Is the fish safe to eat?
faq.a: Yes. Classes use sushi-grade fish that is safe for raw consumption. The instructor covers food safety at the start.

faq.q: What types of sushi will I make?
faq.a: Most beginner classes focus on maki rolls and simple nigiri. Check the event listing for specifics.

faq.q: Can I take leftover sushi home?
faq.a: Most classes let you take home what you made. Sushi is best eaten fresh, but it keeps for a few hours.
